# synology-apm plan — Protection / Retirement / Tiering Plans

APM has three plan types:

- **Protection plans** define backup schedules and retention; assigned to active
  Machine or M365 workloads via `synology-apm machine change-plan` /
  `synology-apm m365 <type> change-plan`.
- **Retirement (Archive) plans** define what happens to data after a workload is
  retired; assigned at retire time (`synology-apm machine retire --plan ...` /
  `synology-apm m365 <type> retire --plan ...`) or re-assigned later via the same
  `change-plan` commands.
- **Tiering plans** define storage tiering policies (read-only via this CLI — list
  and get only).

See [apm-shared](../apm-shared/SKILL.md) for global options, output formats, and
confirmation rules.

## Commands

### `synology-apm plan protection list`

List Protection Plans, optionally filtered by workload category.

**Examples:**

```sh
synology-apm plan protection list                   # list all (machine + m365)
synology-apm plan protection list --category machine  # Machine Plans only
synology-apm plan protection list --category m365     # M365 Plans only
synology-apm plan protection list -v                # show Description column
```

```
Usage: synology-apm plan protection list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--category, -c <CATEGORY>` | Workload category filter: machine / m365 (omit for all) |
| `--search, -s <SEARCH>` | Name keyword search |

### `synology-apm plan protection get`

Show details for a Protection Plan.

**Examples:**

```sh
synology-apm plan protection get "Daily Backup"                           # name search
synology-apm plan protection get --id 0c8f033b-fb57-4f46-9a9d-85e9d21c08ab  # exact lookup
```

```
Usage: synology-apm plan protection get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Plan name (search mode) |
| `--id <PLAN_ID>` | Plan ID (direct mode) |

### `synology-apm plan retirement list`

List all Retirement Plans.

```
Usage: synology-apm plan retirement list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--search, -s <SEARCH>` | Name keyword search |

### `synology-apm plan retirement get`

Show details for a Retirement Plan.

**Examples:**

```sh
synology-apm plan retirement get "Compliance Retention"                 # name search
synology-apm plan retirement get --id cc39711f-deb9-40fa-b6c4-27ca82958d3c  # exact lookup
```

```
Usage: synology-apm plan retirement get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Plan name (search mode) |
| `--id <PLAN_ID>` | Plan ID (direct mode) |

### `synology-apm plan tiering list`

List all Tiering Plans.

```
Usage: synology-apm plan tiering list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--search, -s <SEARCH>` | Name keyword search |

### `synology-apm plan tiering get`

Show details for a Tiering Plan.

**Examples:**

```sh
synology-apm plan tiering get "My Tiering Plan"                          # name search
synology-apm plan tiering get --id f56f8969-a831-47a6-9de0-279696dafea6  # exact lookup
```

```
Usage: synology-apm plan tiering get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Plan name (search mode) |
| `--id <PLAN_ID>` | Plan ID (direct mode) |

## Tips

- To assign or change the plan on a workload, use `synology-apm machine change-plan`
  / `synology-apm m365 <type> change-plan` — the `--plan` value is resolved against
  Protection plans for an active workload, or Retirement plans for an already-retired
  one (`synology-apm machine retire` / `synology-apm m365 <type> retire` first).
- Use `--verbose`/`-v` on `protection list` to see the Description column, and on
  `retirement list`/`tiering list` to see the Plan ID needed for `--id`/`--plan`.
